title: ACPM Capability Profile
description: Agent Capability and Profile Model v1 (SC-014) — a portable, vendor-neutral 'spec sheet' describing what an AI agent, platform, tool, or model actually offers: capability support level, trust/attestation, cost, SLA, and delegation rules. Lets registries, marketplaces, and orchestrators compare and match agents/platforms/tools/models without bespoke per-vendor parsing.

^x-.description: Reserved extension namespace (SPECIFICATION §H/§W-equivalent). Any property whose name begins with 'x-' carries vendor- or tool-specific metadata. Conforming consumers MUST ignore extension keys they do not understand and MUST NOT let them change capability, trust, or cost decisions. The validator hint 'x-sc-schema' (naming the target schema file) lives in this namespace.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: A routing, gating, or filter condition. Two forms are accepted. The STRUCTURED object form is deterministic and portable — every conforming consumer evaluates it identically — and is the RECOMMENDED form. The bare STRING form is a natural-language hint only: it is non-deterministic, each consumer interprets it independently, and it MUST NOT be relied on for portable evaluation. Copied verbatim (semantics unchanged) from SC-006 AAIF SPECIFICATION §V.

oneOf.description: Natural-language hint (non-deterministic). Provided for human readability and best-effort routing. Consumers SHOULD log a portability warning when routing on a string condition.

lang.description: Expression language. 'jsonpath'/'jmespath': query evaluated for truthiness against the input/payload; 'cel': Common Expression Language boolean; 'jsonlogic': a JsonLogic rule object; 'always'/'never': constant true/false (no expr needed). Consumers MUST evaluate these deterministically.

expr.description: The expression. A string for jsonpath/jmespath/cel, or an object for jsonlogic. Omitted for 'always'/'never'.

nl.description: Optional natural-language description of the condition for humans and logs. Advisory only — the structured 'expr' is authoritative.

_comment.description: Non-normative human annotation. Ignored by conforming consumers; safe to strip. Use 'x-'-prefixed keys for machine-readable extensions.

sc_standard.description: Schema Commons standard identifier — must be 'SC-014'.

sc_version.description: ACPM schema version this document conforms to.

profile_id.description: Globally unique profile identifier (UUID). Assigned by the authoring party; SHOULD be preserved across re-publication.

subject.description: Identity of the thing this profile describes: an agent, platform, tool, or model.

subject_type.description: What kind of subject this profile describes.

vendor.description: Organisation or individual that produces/operates the subject.

version.description: The subject's own version (independent of sc_version, the ACPM schema version).

homepage.description: Canonical URL for the subject's documentation or product page.

sc_refs.description: Cross-references to other Schema Commons documents this profile describes or relates to (e.g. an SC-006 AAIF agent_id or an SC-013 ARP registry entry id).

id.description: Identifier within that standard's document (e.g. an AAIF agent_id UUID, or an ARP registry entry id).

capabilities.description: What the subject actually supports, and to what degree. Capability ids SHOULD use the same dot-namespaced vocabulary as SC-006 AAIF's required_capabilities[] (e.g. 'tool.mcp', 'memory.vector', 'orchestration.parallel') so AAIF agents and ARP registry entries can match against ACPM profiles without translation.

support.description: 'native': first-class, fully supported; 'emulated': achieved via a workaround or composition of other features; 'partial': supported with material limitations; 'none': not supported (informational — explicitly declaring absence is useful for comparison).

version_constraint.description: Semver range over the subject's own version for which this capability claim holds (e.g. '>=2.0.0').

notes.description: Free-form clarification (limitations, caveats, links to docs).

tools.description: Tool-invocation protocols the subject supports and their operational limits.

protocol.description: Tool invocation protocol, aligned with SC-006 AAIF tools[].protocol.

max_concurrent_calls.description: Maximum number of concurrent tool calls the subject can issue or accept for this protocol.

auth_methods.description: Authentication methods supported for this tool protocol.

models.description: LLMs the subject exposes, routes to, or is itself an instance of (for subject_type='model').

provider.description: LLM provider identifier (free-form string; see SC-006 AAIF model.provider for a recommended vocabulary, e.g. 'openai', 'anthropic', 'google').

model_id.description: Model identifier in the provider's own notation (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-opus-4-5').

context_window.description: Maximum input context window, in tokens.

memory.description: Memory backends the subject supports, and the scopes available on each.

backend.description: Storage backend, aligned with SC-006 AAIF memory[].backend.

scopes.description: Memory scopes available on this backend, aligned with SC-006 AAIF memory[].scope.

trust.description: Trust posture of the subject: how strongly its claims can be relied upon, and what sandboxing applies when it executes.

level.description: Overall trust tier. 'untrusted': no claims verified; 'sandboxed': runs under isolation but unverified; 'verified': self- or third-party-checked against the profile's claims; 'attested': formally attested per the 'attestation' block; 'enterprise': attested plus organisational accountability (SLA, support tier, compliance).

issuer.description: Entity that issued the attestation (e.g. the vendor itself, a named auditor, or a certification body).

evidence_url.description: Link to the attestation report, audit, or supporting evidence.

issued_at.description: When the attestation was issued (ISO 8601 UTC).

expires_at.description: When the attestation expires, if applicable (ISO 8601 UTC).

sandbox.description: Isolation characteristics when the subject executes untrusted workloads.

isolation.description: Strongest isolation boundary the subject runs within.

network_egress.description: Outbound network access policy under that isolation boundary.

included_units.description: Number of billing units included free per period.

period.description: Renewal period for the free allotment.

support_tier.description: Level of vendor support backing this subject.

severity1_minutes.description: Committed response time, in minutes, for a Severity-1 incident.

delegation_rules.description: Rules governing whether the subject may delegate work to, or accept delegated work from, other profiles/capabilities. At least one of 'target.profile_id', 'target.capability_id', or 'target.trust_level_min' SHOULD be present on each rule (non-normative guidance — not enforced structurally so that broad 'allow/deny by direction only' rules remain expressible).

direction.description: 'outbound': this subject delegating to another; 'inbound': another subject delegating to this one.

target.description: What this rule applies to. All fields optional; SHOULD declare at least one.

profile_id.description: Restrict this rule to a specific target/source ACPM profile.

capability_id.description: Restrict this rule to delegation involving a specific capability id.

trust_level_min.description: Minimum trust level the counterparty must hold for this rule to apply.

condition.description: Additional structured (or string-hint) condition gating this rule. Same Condition semantics as SC-006 AAIF SPECIFICATION §V.

data_residency.description: Regions in which the subject can guarantee data is stored/processed.

pii_handling.description: How the subject handles personally identifiable information.

provenance.description: Authorship and integrity metadata for this profile document.

license.description: SPDX license identifier or URL for this profile document (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0').

signature.description: Optional base64-encoded cryptographic signature over the canonical JSON of this document. Advisory only — ACPM does not itself enforce or verify signatures; a consuming platform that chooses to verify it gains correspondingly more assurance. See SECURITY.md.
